title: next_output
description: Schema for the JSON payload emitted on stdout by `/livespec:next` (the spec-side thin-transport ranker), per `SPECIFICATION/contracts.md` §"/livespec:next spec-side thin-transport skill" → "Output schema". Two top-level keys: `candidates` (ALL ripe candidates after offset/limit slicing) and `pagination` (the offset/limit echo plus total + has_more). Pure function of `<spec-target>/proposed_changes/` queue state + `<spec-target>/history/` unpruned version count. No LLM in the ranking path; no impl-side store reads. An empty `candidates` array IS the no-work signal.

candidates.description: Ranked candidate actions, sorted by action tier (prune-history strictly last), urgency descending, then `target` lexicographic, with `--offset`/`--limit` applied last.

action.description: The action this candidate recommends. The enumeration matches the action set in `SPECIFICATION/contracts.md` §"/livespec:next spec-side thin-transport skill" → "Output schema".

reason.description: Human-readable narration of why this candidate is ripe (queue depth, proposal age, history version count).

urgency.description: Bucketed urgency derived from queue depth and per-proposal age. `prune-history` candidates always emit `low`.

target.description: Spec-target-relative path or identifier naming the specific item the candidate refers to (e.g. the proposed_change filename for a `revise` candidate). MAY be omitted when the candidate has no specific target.

total.description: Total count of ripe candidates BEFORE `offset` and `limit` are applied.
